# Calibration evidence — v1.1.0 exhaustiveness contract

Snapshot of live `codex-cli 0.133.0` runs against fixtures f2..f6 immediately
after the v1.1.0 exhaustiveness contract landed (commit `78a5f30`,
2026-05-29). Persisted so the v1.1.0 release line has machine-checkable
proof of behavior — not just a prose summary in `live-codex-calibration-LOG.md`.

## Artifacts

| Fixture | Wrapper | Persisted file(s) |
|---------|---------|-------------------|
| f1-research-contradiction | `codex-validate-research.sh` | (verified inline during `78a5f30`; not persisted to disk) |
| f2-plan-write-overlap | `codex-validate-plan.sh PLAN.md 'PHASE-XX-*.md'` | `f2-plan-write-overlap/calibration-v1.1.0-attempt-1.{json,prompt.txt}` |
| f3-plan-ambiguity | `codex-validate-plan.sh PLAN.md 'PHASE-XX.md'` | `f3-plan-ambiguity/calibration-v1.1.0-attempt-1.{json,prompt.txt}` |
| f4-phase-missing-tests | `codex-review-phase.sh TASK.md PHASE-XX.md engineer-payload.json` | `f4-phase-missing-tests/calibration-v1.1.0-attempt-1.{json,prompt.txt}` |
| f5-phase-style-drift | `codex-review-phase.sh TASK.md PHASE-XX.md engineer-payload.json` | `f5-phase-style-drift/calibration-v1.1.0-attempt-1.{json,prompt.txt}` |
| f6-fix-loop-exhausted | `codex-review-phase.sh` against PATCHED PHASE-XX.md (post-fix re-review) | `f6-fix-loop-exhausted/post-fix-review-attempt-1.json` |

## Per-fixture outcomes

| Fixture | Wrapper exit | Expected ID match | Expected kind match | `why_real_risk` / `repro` populated on substantive | `tests_to_add` on substantive | `not_fully_audited[]` |
|---------|--------------|-------------------|----------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------|-----------------------|
| f1 | 1 ✓ | YES (`ISS-cc94a3aa8710`) | YES | YES (1/1 HIGH + 1/1 MEDIUM) | YES | `[]` (legit full coverage on small fixture) |
| f2 | 1 ✓ | NO (LLM stochasticity on SHA-256 inputs) | YES (`write-scope-overlap` HIGH) | YES | n/a (plan_validator, not phase_reviewer) | TBD per attempt |
| f3 | 1 ✓ | NO (same LLM stochasticity) | YES (`ambiguous-task-wording` variant) | YES | n/a | TBD per attempt |
| f4 | 1 ✓ | NO (LLM stochasticity) | YES (`missing-test*` variant) | YES | YES (6/6 substantive — phase_reviewer takes `tests_to_add` seriously) | TBD per attempt |
| f5 | 1 ✓ | **YES** (`ISS-4653b5e9bc97` exact match) | YES | YES on the 1 substantive | partial | TBD per attempt |
| f6 (post-fix) | 1 ✓ | seeded `ISS-23ba7d51473d` **NOT in findings** ✓ (resolved by self-fix patch) | n/a | n/a — new pass, new findings | YES on substantive | `2` entries (codex honest about budget) |

## Interpretation

### Business correctness — PASS

Every fixture's seeded issue surfaces with the correct `kind` and severity bucket. Wrapper exits are correct (1 when HIGH/MEDIUM found, which is the orchestrator's signal to enter fix-loop).

### Exhaustiveness contract — PASS

Substantive findings count is consistently 1–7 per pass (up from typical 1 pre-v1.1.0). New optional fields (`why_real_risk`, `repro`, `tests_to_add`) are populated on substantive findings across all fixtures — codex now ACTUALLY substantiates findings instead of emitting top-N drive-by issues.

f6 post-fix specifically: 7 substantive findings + 2 `not_fully_audited[]` entries. Before exhaustiveness contract, codex would have emitted "1 HIGH + 4 LOW no-findings" and silently skipped budget-exhausted areas. The `not_fully_audited` entries are visible honesty — orchestrator can surface to user instead of treating silence as full coverage.

### ID determinism — KNOWN LLM-LEVEL DRIFT

The SHA-256 ID derivation rule (CALIB-01) reduces but does NOT fully eliminate LLM stochasticity: only f1 and f5 reproduced their `expected-findings.json` IDs exactly on this run. f2/f3/f4 found the correct issue with the correct `kind` and severity bucket, but codex picked slightly different `file`/`line_range` inputs to the SHA-256, producing a different but still-deterministic ID. The orchestrator's reclassification rule (ZAP-24) catches persistent issues via `(kind, severity)` even when IDs drift across attempts.

This is an LLM-temperature-level concern, not a contract concern. The orchestrator's contract is "you exit 1 when there are HIGH/MEDIUM findings" — that contract is honored 5/5.

### f6 self-fix end-to-end — PASS

The patch generated by `codex-self-fix.sh` (preserved at `f6/.zapili/codex-self-fix-attempt-1.patch`) was re-applied to a clean copy of `PHASE-XX.md`, then `codex-review-phase.sh` was re-run against the patched artifact. The seeded HIGH `ISS-23ba7d51473d` is ABSENT from the post-fix re-review (`post-fix-review-attempt-1.json`). This closes review finding W-4 (the v1.1 audit's "post-fix re-review JSON not persisted" gap) with a machine-checkable artifact rather than prose-only LOG narration.

## How to refresh

```bash
# f2
cd plugins/zapili/tests/fixtures/f2-plan-write-overlap
bash ../../../scripts/codex-validate-plan.sh PLAN.md 'PHASE-XX-*.md'
# ... wait for codex, then move .zapili/plan-validate-attempt-1.{json,prompt.txt}
#     to calibration-v1.1.0-attempt-N.{json,prompt.txt}
cd -

# f3 — same pattern as f2 with PHASE-XX.md (single)
# f4 / f5 — codex-review-phase.sh TASK.md PHASE-XX.md engineer-payload.json
# f6 post-fix — apply f6/.zapili/codex-self-fix-attempt-1.patch in a scratch
#               git repo, then run codex-review-phase.sh on the patched copy
```

Refresh whenever `codex-prompts.md` or any `codex-*.sh` wrapper changes — calibration evidence is the only way to catch contract drift between the prompts we ship and codex's actual behavior.
